title: Crypto-themed X.com auto-reply bot
description:
## Goal

Build a Node.js + TypeScript bot that ingests X.com mentions/replies via polling, generates crypto-themed responses with the OpenAI API, and posts replies back — with safety guardrails and idempotency.

## Architecture

Polling worker (X webhooks/AAA require Enterprise access, so we poll mentions on a schedule). A decoupled reply pipeline (dedupe -> filter -> OpenAI gen -> safety -> post -> persist) is reused by an optional AAA webhook receiver added later.

## Stack

- Node.js 20+, TypeScript
- twitter-api-v2 (X client)
- openai SDK (Chat/Responses + Moderation)
- Fastify (optional webhook + health)
- better-sqlite3 (since_id cursor + replied-ID dedupe)
- zod config, pino logging, node-cron/interval scheduler

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Constraints / Risks

- X Basic tier (~$200/mo) realistically required for reliable read+write.
- Automation/spam enforcement risk -> throttle + quality filters.
- Crypto + AI financial-advice liability -> moderation + disclaimer enforced in code.

title: Scaffold TS project + tooling
description:
## Context

Greenfield Node.js + TypeScript project for the X.com crypto bot. Establish the build/dev tooling so all later beads have a foundation.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] package.json with scripts: dev (tsx/ts-node watch), build (tsc), start, lint, format
- [ ] tsconfig.json (strict, ES2022, NodeNext)
- [ ] Deps installed: typescript, twitter-api-v2, openai, fastify, better-sqlite3, zod, dotenv, pino, node-cron
- [ ] Dev deps: tsx, eslint, prettier, @types/*
- [ ] .env.example with all required vars documented
- [ ] .gitignore (node_modules, dist, .env, *.db)
- [ ] src/index.ts placeholder builds and runs

## Notes

Use NodeNext module resolution. better-sqlite3 is native — note build prerequisites in README later.
close_reason: Approved by inspector. All 7 AC items satisfied; scope clean; no regressions. Guard confirmed lint/build/start/format all green. TS scaffold (package.json, tsconfig, eslint/prettier, .env.example, .gitignore, src/index.ts) ready as foundation for dependent beads.

title: Optional AAA webhook receiver
description:
## Context

Feature-flagged Fastify endpoint for Account Activity API push events, reusing the same pipeline. Off by default (Enterprise-only).

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/server.ts Fastify app with /health
- [ ] src/x/webhook.ts handles AAA CRC challenge (HMAC-SHA256) on GET
- [ ] POST event route normalizes mentions and feeds pipeline
- [ ] Enabled only when WEBHOOK_ENABLED=true
- [ ] Signature verification of inbound events

## Notes

Decoupled so AAA can be enabled later without touching core logic.

title: Entrypoint + graceful shutdown
description:
## Context

Wire everything together and handle lifecycle.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/index.ts loads config, inits store, starts poller
- [ ] Conditionally starts Fastify server when WEBHOOK_ENABLED=true
- [ ] SIGINT/SIGTERM graceful shutdown (stop poller, close DB/server)
- [ ] Single pino logger instance shared

## Notes

Health endpoint should report poller liveness.

title: Dockerfile + deployment
description:
## Context

Containerize the long-running worker for VPS/Fly/Railway deployment.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Multi-stage Dockerfile (build deps for better-sqlite3, slim runtime)
- [ ] Non-root user; persistent volume for SQLite DB
- [ ] HEALTHCHECK against /health (or process check)
- [ ] Honors env-based config; no secrets baked in
- [ ] Image builds and container boots in DRY_RUN

## Notes

Document required native build packages.

- OK: no broken internal anchors; relative links to prompts/persona.md valid.

An operator copying it would fail validation. This README is correct against config.ts; the drift lives in the env/config artifacts, not this bead. Filing a follow-up bead so it isn't lost.

### Good Practices
- Genuinely operator-grade docs: ASCII flow diagram, tier table, conservative-defaults guidance, and a thorough troubleshooting matrix that maps real failure strings to fixes.
- Strong responsible-automation section that reinforces the financial-advice guardrail at the policy level.

**Verdict: approve**

title: Config loader with zod-validated env
description:
## Context

Central typed config validated at startup so the app fails fast on missing/invalid env.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/config.ts exports a validated, typed config object (zod)
- [ ] Validates X creds: X_APP_KEY, X_APP_SECRET, X_ACCESS_TOKEN, X_ACCESS_SECRET, BOT_USER_ID
- [ ] Validates OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_MODEL (default gpt-4o-mini), OPENAI_TEMPERATURE
- [ ] Validates POLL_INTERVAL_SEC, MAX_REPLIES_PER_RUN, DRY_RUN (bool), PERSONA_PROMPT or PERSONA_PROMPT_PATH
- [ ] Throws a clear aggregated error listing all missing/invalid vars
- [ ] No secrets logged

## Notes

Load .env via dotenv only outside production; allow real env in prod.
close_reason: Approved by inspector. All 6 AC items satisfied by src/config.ts (zod-validated typed config, X+OpenAI+behavior env validation, aggregated secret-free error, dotenv only outside prod). Diff scope clean (config.ts only). Guard CI green (lint/format/build). No critical issues.

title: SQLite store: cursor + reply dedupe
description:
## Context

Persistence for idempotency: track the since_id cursor and the set of source tweet IDs already replied to, surviving restarts/crashes.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/store/db.ts initializes better-sqlite3 with schema (migrations on boot)
- [ ] getCursor()/setCursor(sinceId) for mentions pagination
- [ ] hasReplied(tweetId) / markReplied(tweetId) idempotency helpers
- [ ] DB path configurable; created if absent
- [ ] Prepared statements; no SQL injection

## Notes

Single-writer assumption (one worker). Keep schema minimal.
close_reason: Approved by inspector. All 5 AC satisfied by src/store/db.ts: better-sqlite3 with boot migrations, getCursor/setCursor, idempotent hasReplied/markReplied (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), configurable DB path created if absent, fully parameterized prepared statements (no injection). Diff scope clean (db.ts only). Guard CI green. No critical issues.

comments.author: worker
comments.text: [village] worktree: /Users/technoch1ef/Repositories/xbot

comments.author: worker
comments.text:
[handoff worker→guard] Implementation complete. Ready for CI checks.

Added src/store/db.ts exporting `openStore(dbPath?)` -> Store with:
- getCursor()/setCursor(sinceId) backed by a key/value `meta` table (since_id key)
- hasReplied(tweetId)/markReplied(tweetId) backed by `replied_tweets` table; markReplied is idempotent (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING)
- Migrations run on boot via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS (idempotent)
- DB path configurable (defaults to DATABASE_PATH env, fallback ./xbot.db); parent dirs created via mkdirSync recursive; ":memory:" handled
- All queries are prepared statements with bound params (no SQL injection); WAL mode enabled
- close() to release the handle

Verified locally: prettier clean, eslint clean, `tsc --noEmit` passes. Did not run tests (guard's job).

comments.author: inspector
comments.text: [village] worktree: /Users/technoch1ef/Repositories/xbot

comments.author: technoch1ef
comments.text:
**Inspector judgment for bead bd-120.3**

### AC coverage
- [x] src/store/db.ts initializes better-sqlite3 with schema (migrations on boot) — migrate() runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS at openStore (db.ts:47-51,85-96)
- [x] getCursor()/setCursor(sinceId) — kv `meta` table, since_id key (db.ts:65-71)
- [x] hasReplied()/markReplied() idempotency — markReplied uses ON CONFLICT(tweet_id) DO NOTHING (db.ts:59-62,72-77)
- [x] DB path configurable; created if absent — dbPath defaults to DATABASE_PATH env / ./xbot.db; mkdirSync recursive for parent dir; ":memory:" special-cased (db.ts:40-45)
- [x] Prepared statements; no SQL injection — all 4 statements prepared with bound `?` params, zero string interpolation (db.ts:53-62)

### Diff scope
- OK: commit d588f18 touched only src/store/db.ts (+ .beads tracking). No scope creep.

### Regression sniff
- OK: no console.log, no swallowed errors. WAL + foreign_keys pragmas set. close() releases handle.

### Skill review
- OK: village-workflow — no convention violations.

### Critical Issues
- None.

### Suggestions (non-blocking, for mayor/future beads)
- DATABASE_PATH is read directly via process.env here rather than the validated config from bd-120.2. Acceptable since AC says 'DB path configurable' and openStore() accepts an explicit param the orchestrator (bd-120.8) can wire from config. Consider centralizing in config later for consistency.

### Good Practices
- Clean typed `Store` interface separates contract from implementation.
- Idempotent migrations + idempotent markReplied satisfy crash/restart durability.
- Parameterized statements prepared once at open for both safety and performance.

**Verdict: approve**

title: X API client wrapper + rate-limit handling
description:
## Context

Thin wrapper over twitter-api-v2 providing the only X operations the bot needs, with backoff for 429s and rate-limit awareness.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/x/client.ts authenticates with OAuth 1.0a user context (post) using configured creds
- [ ] getMentions(sinceId?) returns new mentions with author + text + conversation context
- [ ] reply(text, inReplyToTweetId) posts a reply
- [ ] Handles 429 with exponential backoff honoring rate-limit reset headers
- [ ] Typed return shapes; errors surfaced, not swallowed

## Notes

Use expansions/fields to fetch author username + referenced tweet for context.
close_reason: Approved by inspector. All 5 AC satisfied: OAuth 1.0a user-context auth, getMentions with author/conversation context, reply, 429 handling honoring rate-limit reset headers with exponential backoff fallback, and typed returns with non-swallowed errors. Diff scoped to src/x/client.ts only; no regression patterns. Guard CI green (build/lint/format).

comments.author: worker
comments.text: [village] worktree: /Users/technoch1ef/Repositories/xbot

comments.author: worker
comments.text:
[handoff worker→guard] Implementation complete. Added src/x/client.ts: createXClient factory over twitter-api-v2 with OAuth 1.0a user-context auth.

- getMentions(sinceId?): userMentionTimeline for BOT_USER_ID with expansions/fields (author_id, referenced_tweets, conversation_id); maps to typed Mention[] with authorUsername + inReplyToText via includes helper.
- reply(text, inReplyToTweetId): posts reply, returns typed ReplyResult.
- withRateLimitRetry: handles 429 by honoring rateLimit.reset header (sleep until reset + 1s buffer) with capped exponential backoff fallback; throws after maxRetries. Non-429 ApiResponseErrors wrapped in typed XApiError (code preserved); unknown errors rethrown — nothing swallowed.
- Injection seams (api/sleep/retry) for testability.

Verified locally: `npm run build` (tsc) passes, `npm run lint` clean, `npm run format` no changes. Did not run tests (guard's job). Ready for CI checks.

comments.text: [village] worktree: /Users/technoch1ef/Repositories/xbot

comments.author: technoch1ef
comments.text:
**Inspector judgment for bead bd-120.4**

### AC coverage
- [x] OAuth 1.0a user-context auth — createXClient constructs TwitterApi with appKey/appSecret/accessToken/accessSecret from validated Config (all 5 fields confirmed present in src/config.ts)
- [x] getMentions(sinceId?) with author + text + conversation context — userMentionTimeline for BOT_USER_ID with expansions/fields; maps to typed Mention[] with authorUsername, conversationId, inReplyToText
- [x] reply(text, inReplyToTweetId) — posts via api.v2.reply, returns typed ReplyResult
- [x] 429 with exponential backoff honoring reset headers — withRateLimitRetry honors error.rateLimit.reset (epoch s) with +1s skew buffer; falls back to capped exponential backoff clamped to maxDelayMs
- [x] Typed returns; errors surfaced — Mention/ReplyResult/XClient interfaces; non-429 ApiResponseError wrapped in XApiError (code preserved), unknown errors rethrown — nothing swallowed

### Regression sniff

### Skill review

### Good Practices
- Thorough JSDoc throughout; clock-skew buffer on rate-limit waits; DI seams (api/sleep/retry) make the rate-limit path unit-testable without network.

**Verdict: approve**

title: OpenAI crypto reply generation
description:
## Context

Generate a crypto-themed reply from an incoming mention using the OpenAI SDK with a configurable persona system prompt.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/ai/generate.ts builds messages from persona system prompt + incoming tweet (+ limited thread context)
- [ ] Persona loaded from PERSONA_PROMPT or PERSONA_PROMPT_PATH (default in prompts/persona.md)
- [ ] Output constrained to <=280 chars (instruction + hard truncation fallback)
- [ ] Model/temperature from config
- [ ] Returns generated text + token usage for logging

## Notes

Default persona: knowledgeable, witty crypto-native voice. Never gives financial advice (enforced separately in safety layer).
close_reason: Approved by inspector. All 5 AC satisfied: persona-anchored message building with thread context, persona resolution chain (PERSONA_PROMPT → path → default), 280-char enforcement via instruction + hard truncation, model/temperature from config, and text+token-usage returns. Scoped to src/ai/generate.ts + prompts/persona.md; no regressions. One non-blocking suggestion noted re: unreachable default-persona fallback (belongs to config bead). Guard CI green.

title: Safety guardrails (moderation + disclaimer)
description:
## Context

Enforce safety on generated replies regardless of persona prompt: financial-advice liability and content moderation.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/pipeline/safety.ts runs OpenAI Moderation on generated text; block on flag
- [ ] Strips/blocks explicit financial-advice phrasing; appends short disclaimer when needed
- [ ] Hard char-limit enforcement (<=280) after any modification
- [ ] Profanity/spam guard
- [ ] Returns { ok, text, reason? }

## Notes

Guardrails live in code, not just the prompt, so they cannot be bypassed by persona config.
close_reason: Approved by inspector. All 5 AC satisfied: OpenAI Moderation (fail-closed block on flag), financial-advice blocking + disclaimer append, hard 280-char re-enforcement via enforceLength, profanity/spam guards, and { ok, text, reason? } return shape. Diff scope clean (only safety.ts + minor .gitignore). No regressions. Two non-blocking suggestions noted (broken JSDoc link, empty-input short-circuit).

Returns { ok, text, reason? }. Pure helpers (hasProhibitedAdvice, needsDisclaimer, hasProfanity, looksLikeSpam, appendDisclaimer, applyLocalGuards) exported for testability. comments.text: [village] worktree: /Users/technoch1ef/Repositories/xbot

comments.author: technoch1ef
comments.text:
**Inspector judgment for bead bd-120.6**

### AC coverage
- [x] OpenAI Moderation on generated text, block on flag — createSafetyGuard.check() runs moderation first (fail-closed), returns { ok:false, reason:"moderation-flagged" } on any flagged result (safety.ts:215-222).
- [x] Strips/blocks explicit financial-advice phrasing; appends disclaimer when needed — hasProhibitedAdvice hard-blocks guaranteed/risk-free phrasing (safety.ts:71-114); needsDisclaimer+appendDisclaimer append terse DISCLAIMER for buy/sell/invest cues (safety.ts:85-92,156-162,188).
- [x] Hard char-limit <=280 after any modification — applyLocalGuards re-runs enforceLength after disclaimer append (safety.ts:191); enforceLength caps at MAX_TWEET_CHARS=280.
- [x] Profanity/spam guard — hasProfanity slur blocklist + looksLikeSpam structural heuristics (safety.ts:100-150).
- [x] Returns { ok, text, reason? } — SafetyResult interface returned by all paths (safety.ts:32-43).

### Diff scope
- OK: src/pipeline/safety.ts is the expected deliverable.
- Minor FLAG: .gitignore adds .bv/ — outside AC but harmless hygiene, not blocking.

### Regression sniff
- OK: no console.log/debug, no swallowed errors. Moderation is fail-closed (errors propagate rather than allowing unvetted text) — correct security posture per the Notes.

### Skill review
- OK: pure helpers exported for testability; clean separation between network (moderation) and local guards; strong typing throughout.

### Suggestions — non-blocking
- safety.ts:171 — JSDoc {@link MAX_TWEET_CHARS} references a symbol not imported into this file, so the link will not resolve. Import the constant or inline 280 chars.
- Empty/whitespace-only candidate is still sent to moderation.create; consider short-circuiting empty input before the network call.

### Good Practices
- Code-level guardrails that cannot be bypassed by persona config — matches bead Notes intent exactly.
- Fail-closed moderation and correct order-of-operations (block then disclaimer then re-trim), well-documented.

**Verdict: approve**

title: Inbound mention filter
description:
## Context

Decide which mentions deserve a reply before spending OpenAI tokens.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/pipeline/filter.ts skips the bot's own tweets and retweets
- [ ] Skips already-replied IDs (via store)
- [ ] Optional blocklist of user IDs/handles from config
- [ ] Optional language filter
- [ ] Pure/testable predicate returning a skip reason

## Notes

Keep filtering cheap and side-effect free.
close_reason: Approved by inspector. All 5 AC satisfied: skips own tweets/retweets, already-replied via store, optional blocklist (IDs + handles), optional language gate, and a pure side-effect-free predicate returning machine-readable SkipReason. Scoped to filter.ts + config (BLOCKLIST/ALLOWED_LANGUAGES) + .env.example; no regressions. Retweet/lang predicates correctly implemented; field enrichment owned by orchestrator bd-120.8. Guard CI green.

comments.author: worker
comments.text: [village] worktree: /Users/technoch1ef/Repositories/xbot

comments.author: worker
comments.text:
[handoff worker→guard] Implementation complete. Added src/pipeline/filter.ts; added optional BLOCKLIST and ALLOWED_LANGUAGES to config (src/config.ts) + .env.example.

- shouldReply(mention, options, store): pure, side-effect-free predicate returning { reply, reason? } with machine-readable SkipReason ('own-tweet' | 'retweet' | 'already-replied' | 'blocked-user' | 'unsupported-language'). Checks ordered cheapest-first; only external read is store.hasReplied.
- Skips: bot's own tweets (authorId === BOT_USER_ID), retweets (isRetweet), blocked users (by numeric ID or @handle), unsupported language (when ALLOWED_LANGUAGES set), already-replied (via store).
- buildFilterOptions(config): normalizes BLOCKLIST (digits → IDs, else handles w/ @ stripped, lowercased) and ALLOWED_LANGUAGES into sets.
- ReplyDedupe is a narrow read-only interface; the existing Store (src/store/db.ts) satisfies it structurally.

Plumbing note for orchestrator (bd-120.8): FilterInput accepts optional isRetweet and lang. The current Mention from src/x/client.ts (bd-120.4) does not yet carry those two fields, so retweet/language gating only engages once the orchestrator populates them (e.g. by enriching the client mapping from TweetV2.referenced_tweets[type=retweeted] and TweetV2.lang). All other checks work with Mention as-is.

Verified locally: `npm run build` (tsc) passes, `npm run lint` clean, `npm run format` no changes. Did not run tests. Ready for CI checks.

comments.text: [village] worktree: /Users/technoch1ef/Repositories/xbot

comments.author: technoch1ef
comments.text:
**Inspector judgment for bead bd-120.7**

### AC coverage
- [x] Skips bot's own tweets and retweets — own-tweet (authorId===botUserId) and retweet (isRetweet===true) checks
- [x] Skips already-replied IDs via store — store.hasReplied(mention.id), narrow read-only ReplyDedupe interface
- [x] Optional blocklist of user IDs/handles — buildFilterOptions partitions CSV into blockedUserIds (all-digit) vs blockedUsernames (@-stripped, lowercased); checked by ID or handle
- [x] Optional language filter — allowedLanguages gate engages only when non-empty
- [x] Pure/testable predicate returning skip reason — shouldReply is side-effect free (only external read is hasReplied), returns machine-readable SkipReason union

### Diff scope
- OK: src/pipeline/filter.ts + config.ts (BLOCKLIST/ALLOWED_LANGUAGES, both optional) + .env.example + .beads. Config additions are directly required by AC items 3 & 4. All in scope.

### Regression sniff
- OK: no console.log, no mutations, no any-casts. Checks ordered cheapest-first with the store read last. Regex /^\d+$/ correctly classifies IDs vs handles.

### Skill review
- OK: follows village-workflow; clean local commit 30abe9e on epic/xbot, no pushes.

### Plumbing note (acknowledged, not a defect)
- Worker correctly disclosed that isRetweet and lang are not yet carried by Mention (bd-120.4); the retweet/language predicates are implemented and correct but only engage once the orchestrator (bd-120.8) enriches those fields. The downstream bead's AC owns that wiring. This bead's predicate logic is complete.

### Suggestions (non-blocking)
- The comment on step 5 says 'cheapest external read last' — it reads slightly contradictory (it's ordered last because it's the most expensive). Pure wording nit.

### Good Practices
- Thorough JSDoc and a machine-readable SkipReason union that makes drop-logging trivial.
- Narrow ReplyDedupe interface keeps the filter decoupled from the concrete Store (structural compatibility).
- All helpers exported (parseCsv via buildFilterOptions, shouldReply) for straightforward unit testing.

**Verdict: approve**

title: Reply pipeline orchestrator
description:
## Context

Orchestrate per-tweet processing, reused by both poller and optional webhook ingestion.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/pipeline/process.ts: filter -> generate -> safety -> post -> markReplied
- [ ] Respects DRY_RUN (log intended reply, do not post)
- [ ] Respects MAX_REPLIES_PER_RUN throttle
- [ ] Structured pino logging per stage with tweet IDs + token usage
- [ ] Errors on one tweet do not abort the batch

## Notes

Ingestion-agnostic: accepts a normalized mention object.

title: Polling worker (scheduler)
description:
## Context

Primary ingestion: periodically fetch new mentions and feed the pipeline, advancing the cursor.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/x/poller.ts loops on POLL_INTERVAL_SEC (interval or node-cron)
- [ ] Fetches mentions since stored cursor, processes via pipeline
- [ ] Advances cursor only after successful batch handling
- [ ] Backoff on rate-limit; never overlapping runs
- [ ] Graceful start/stop

## Notes

Guard against overlapping ticks if a run exceeds the interval.

title: .env.example out of sync with config schema
description:
## Context

Discovered during inspector review of bd-120.13 (README docs). The shipped `.env.example` does not match the validated schema in `src/config.ts`. An operator copying `.env.example` to `.env` would fail startup validation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Remove stale keys not read by config.ts: X_BEARER_TOKEN, BOT_HANDLE, SEARCH_QUERY, POLL_CRON, PORT, HOST, LOG_LEVEL, NODE_ENV (or justify each)
- [ ] Add missing keys used by config.ts: BOT_USER_ID, OPENAI_TEMPERATURE, POLL_INTERVAL_SEC, DRY_RUN, PERSONA_PROMPT and/or PERSONA_PROMPT_PATH
- [ ] Ensure .env.example matches the env var table in README.md and src/config.ts (single source of truth)
- [ ] Note the exactly-one-of PERSONA_PROMPT / PERSONA_PROMPT_PATH requirement

## Notes

Parent epic: bd-120. README (bd-120.13) is already correct against config.ts; only .env.example needs reconciliation.
